KOD, +2.97% plummeted 56.9% into record-low territory, and to pace all premarket declines, after the California-based biotechnology company said it decided to discontinue development of wet age-related macular degeneration (AMD) treatment tarcocimab. The decision comes after tarcocimab failed to meet primary efficacy endpoints in two studies of patients on longer treatment intervals, with an unexpected increase in cataracts observed over time.
